vlEvidenceBaseline.pepinoFalseDeflation: -99.95% (conserva jar → fresh kg), OI shows -100% opportunity

options.name: Single ingredient_id only (status quo)
options.description: One ingredients row holds name, current_price, purchase_quantity, base_unit. All history, recipes, OI keyed on ingredient_id.

options.name: Ingredient + Pack Variant
options.description: ingredients = culinary concept; ingredient_pack_variants hold purchase contract (pq, base_unit, current_price). History and invoice match resolve to pack_variant_id. Recipes bind to concept with optional preferred_variant_id.

solvesVLFailures.gingerBeer: partial — needs volume token on variant label

gaps: No explicit supplier-product dimension — same variant label from two suppliers collides
Supplier switch intel still heuristic without product layer
options.verdict: VIABLE Phase 1 foundation — insufficient alone for multi-supplier product intelligence

options.name: Ingredient + Supplier Product
options.description: ingredients = concept; supplier_products = (supplier_id, concept_id, commercial_description). Single price per product — pack format embedded in product row.

solvesVLFailures.mozzarella: partial — Bocconcino vs Aviludo split by supplier product, but piece vs block same supplier still collides

gaps: Pack format changes (case vs unit) require new supplier_product row
History equivalence still ambiguous when one product has multiple purchase formats
options.verdict: GOOD for supplier naming — incomplete for pack-format equivalence

options.name: Ingredient + Supplier Product + Pack Variant
options.description: Three-level: concept → supplier_product (who sells what SKU) → pack_variant (how it is bought: pq, base_unit, price). price_history keyed on pack_variant_id. Aliases map invoice text → supplier_product or pack_variant.
options.verdict: STRONG practical target — balances correctness and deliverability

options.name: Ingredient Concept + Product Layer + Pack Variant Layer
options.description: Explicit culinary concept (recipe ontology) separate from commercial product identity and purchase pack contract. Adds equivalence_groups for substitutable variants, form_dimension (fresh/preserved/processed), and forecasting hooks. Supplier product optional cross-supplier canonical product bridge.
extensions: equivalence_groups for recipe substitution without false price chain
canonical_product bridge for same SKU multiple distributors
options.verdict: RECOMMENDED north-star — implement via phased path that lands D first, then E extensions

recommendedArchitecture.practicalDeliveryPath: E via D-core phases (Phase 0 guard → Phase 1 B-layer → Phase 2 D-layer → Phase 3 E extensions)
justification: VL proves single-ID collapse poisons OI (+1341%, -100%, €744/mo false opportunity) — status quo (A) is disqualified
B fixes pack-format chaining but not supplier-product intelligence requirements
D satisfies all 7 design requirements with acceptable complexity
E adds equivalence_groups and form ontology needed for forecasting and prep substitution without false deltas
Phased delivery avoids big-bang migration; Phase 0 guard stops bleeding before schema work
recommendedArchitecture.whyNotA: Proven false opportunities in production OI replay
recommendedArchitecture.whyNotBAlone: Multi-supplier same-SKU and supplier switch intel need product layer
recommendedArchitecture.whyNotCAlone: Pack format equivalence still breaks without variant layer
recommendedArchitecture.whyDvsE: D is sufficient for production trust; E extensions (equivalence, form, forecast) are Phase 3 not launch blockers

layers.role: Recipe costing anchor, menu ontology, prep leaf reference
Purchase contract — sole key for price_history
Optional substitutable set for recipes and supplier switch (E extension)

targetEntityModel.historyRule: price_history.pack_variant_id NOT NULL; previous_price chains only within same pack_variant_id
targetEntityModel.aliasRule: ingredient_aliases → (supplier_product_id | pack_variant_id) + format snapshot at confirm time
targetEntityModel.recipeRule: recipe_ingredients.ingredient_concept_id + optional pack_variant_id override; sub_recipe_id unchanged

mozzarella.today: 2a99cecd single ID; Bocconcino tray vs Aviludo 2kg block chained
mozzarella.future: One concept 'Mozzarella fior di latte'; two supplier_products; two pack_variants (piece-tray pq=10, block pq=2000g); history never crosses variants
mozzarella.oiImpact: Opportunity compares block-to-block only; supplier watch shows real +4% not +1341%

pepino.future: Two concepts OR one concept with form_dimension=preserved vs fresh blocking match; separate pack_variants; alias cannot cross form

gingerBeer.future: pack_variant.volume_ml_per_unit=200 (20cl) required for beverage; OCR disambiguation creates variant before match; extraction guard rejects implausible ml/unit

aliasContamination.future: alias → pack_variant_id with confirmed contract_hash; re-match creates new variant instead of overwriting

recipeAndPrepImpact.recipeCosting: Recipes bind to ingredient_concept; costing uses default_pack_variant or explicit variant on line. Cascading cost unchanged for sub_recipe_id graph.
recipeAndPrepImpact.prepSubRecipes: Prep batches remain recipes(id) referenced by sub_recipe_id; leaf lines use concept_id. Prep cost rolls up from variant prices on leaf pack_variants.
recipeAndPrepImpact.cascading: recipe-merge walk unchanged structurally; resolveOperationalIngredientCost reads variant price not concept aggregate
recipeAndPrepImpact.migrationUX: Existing recipe_ingredients keep working via default_pack_variant backfill per concept

supplierIntelligenceImpact.perProduct: supplier_product level spend, stability, switch candidates
supplierIntelligenceImpact.vlFix: AVILUDO +1341% note becomes block-variant +2% or format_change flag, not spike
